reforms.description: The R&D tax incentive for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) was extended for an additional two years, alongside a permanent 10% increase in the tax subsidy for R&D, which is estimated to raise R&D spending significantly. Additionally, the 'SME fund' was established to promote investment in SMEs as part of an action plan aimed at enhancing innovation and productivity in service industries.
reforms.component_name: R&D Tax Incentives and SME Fund
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to enhance R&D investment and innovation among SMEs, which is expected to boost productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax incentive extension and the establishment of the SME fund have been put into practice.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the support mechanisms for SMEs but do not constitute a major overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: A permanent 10% increase in the tax subsidy for R&D was estimated to raise the level of R&D spending by over 8%.

reforms.description: The amount of competitive grants for public R&D funds increased, raising its share in central government outlays on science and technology, thereby enhancing the funding landscape for research activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing competitive grants is expected to stimulate research activities and innovation within public research institutions.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in competitive grants has been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in competitive grants is a significant adjustment to the funding structure but does not represent a fundamental change.
reforms.source_quote: The amount of competitive grants rose from 471 billion yen in FY2006 to 477 billion yen in FY2007.

reforms.description: The Japanese government launched the 'New Industry Development Strategy 2005' to accelerate the development of the service sector, focusing on priority areas such as business services, software contents, health/welfare, and environment/energy. Additionally, the 'Program for Enhancing Growth Potential' was initiated to boost productivity growth across all industries, with a specific focus on raising productivity in the service sector.
reforms.component_name: New Industry Development Strategy and Growth Potential Program
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are aimed at enhancing productivity and innovation in the service sector, which is crucial for overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives have been successfully implemented as part of the government's strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms represent a major strategic shift in focusing on the service sector's development and productivity enhancement.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Program for Enhancing Growth Potential' presented by the Council on Economic and Fiscal Policy (CEFP) in 2007 aims to boost productivity growth.

reforms.description: The 'Special Zones for Structural Reform' initiative allows local areas to test regulatory reforms that can later be implemented nationwide, aiming to revitalize regional economies through deregulation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to stimulate local economies and innovation through regulatory flexibility and experimentation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been actively implemented in various local areas.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant structural reform in regulatory practices at the local level.
reforms.source_quote: The programme for 'Special Zones for Structural Reform' began in 2002; it marked the start of a fundamentally new approach in Japan.

reforms.description: Japan replaced the Large-scale Retail Store Law with the Large-scale Retail Store Location Law, shifting regulatory responsibility to local governments and eliminating the objective of balancing supply and demand.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to enhance the startup ecosystem by allowing more flexibility in retail operations, thus fostering innovation and competition.
reforms.status_evidence: The new law has been successfully implemented and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The replacement of the law represents a significant change in the regulatory framework affecting retail and startups.
reforms.source_quote: The Large-scale Retail Store Law was replaced in 2000 by the 'Large-scale Retail Store Location Law', which is aimed at protecting the local living environment.

reforms.description: The MEXT launched a project to promote the diversification of the career path for researchers, enhancing their mobility within public research institutes. Additionally, various strategies have been implemented to develop the business services sector, including setting skill standards and improving infrastructure to facilitate outsourcing through IT.
reforms.package_name: Researcher Mobility and Business Services Development
reforms.component_name: Career Path Diversification and Business Services Strategies
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to enhance the skills and mobility of researchers, which is essential for fostering innovation and improving the business services sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives have been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these initiatives are important for improving human capital, they do not represent a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The government has implemented various strategies to develop this sector, including it as one of six priority sectors in the recent initiative for the service industry.
